purpose: Minimal POSIX launcher script intended to be double-clicked or executed from the Synapse repo root. Changes to its own directory, runs `npm install` if `node_modules/` is missing, then runs `npm run dev` (Vite watch + Electron concurrent). Used as the simplest possible 'just open Synapse' affordance; comments tell first-time macOS users to right-click → Open if Gatekeeper blocks the file.
gotchas: Calls `npm run dev` (NOT `npm start`) — so users who launch via this script get the dev-mode Vite server with hot reload, NOT the production-built UI. May surprise users expecting `npm start` behavior.
Doesn't source the user's shell profile — assumes `npm` and `node` are already on PATH, which fails for users whose Node is installed via nvm or Homebrew without a global shim.
No port-busy check — running this while Synapse is already up will spawn duplicate processes (compare to start.sh which checks port 3456).
First-time `npm install` runs in the foreground without progress; the user only sees a single 'installing dependencies...' line for what may take minutes.
Not chmod +x by default in the file system check — it's `-rw-r--r--` per the ls listing, so it must be invoked as `bash launch.sh` rather than `./launch.sh` until the user fixes permissions.
patterns: Launch sequence pattern: `cd $(dirname $0)` → ensure deps → exec dev/start command.
Lazy install pattern: only run `npm install` when `node_modules/` is missing — keeps subsequent launches fast.
conventions: Comments explain UX (right-click → Open) for non-developer users.
Quoted `"$(dirname "$0")"` to handle paths with spaces.
